adds.rationale: IonQ is the only US-listed pure-play trapped-ion quantum computer company; Q1 2023 revenue of $4.3M beat guidance and doubled YoY, the full-year outlook was raised, and IonQ Forte hit the firm's primary 2023 technical target (29 algorithmic qubits) seven months early — a concrete commercial milestone in a named priority wave (quantum) that currently holds zero watchlist slots.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ Q1 2023 revenue of $4.3M exceeded guidance and more than doubled YoY; full-year 2023 outlook raised to $18.8-19.2M; new UAE Quantum Research Center contract.
IonQ Forte achieved 29 algorithmic qubits (#AQ29), meeting its primary 2023 technical milestone seven months early; IonQ Aria 2 construction completed.
IonQ scheduled to report Q1 2023 results on May 11, 2023, confirming commercial cadence ahead of the rebalance.

removes.rationale: SPY maps to no named wave (general_markets) and occupies one of eight slots at the hard cap; with quantum explicitly named as a priority next wave and holding zero slots, SPY is the least-thesis-aligned position to release.

news_evidence.summary: NVIDIA Q1 FY2024: record data-center revenue $4.28B and Q2 guidance of ~$11B confirm the AI wave in full adoption-surge; the four AI slots are well-covered, so a broad-market catch-all adds no incremental wave value.
Apple WWDC 2023 confirmed a 'deliberate' AI stance with no generative-AI product, underscoring the AI bucket's pure-plays are captured and SPY's slot is better used for quantum entry.

rationale_overall: The watchlist enters Q2 2023 full (8/8) with a clear quantum gap. The surgical fix swaps out SPY (a broad-market catch-all with no wave alignment) for IONQ, the only US-listed pure-play trapped-ion quantum computer company, which beat Q1 2023 guidance, raised its outlook, and hit its primary technical milestone early. Post-change covers AI, rockets, robotics, nuclear, and quantum with no catch-all consuming capacity and no bucket exceeding the four-ticker limit.
